title: GitLab MCP over docker with a personal-access-token env → REVIEW

origin.description: Public documentation config from modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived (src/gitlab/README.md, Docker variant). Content verbatim except that the upstream README annotates the GITLAB_API_URL env with a trailing `// Optional ...` JavaScript-style comment, which is not valid JSON; the comment was removed so the snapshot is a valid JSON document. Both env keys, the docker command, and the image are preserved exactly — the verdict-relevant shape is unchanged.
redactions: removed a non-JSON trailing comment (`// Optional, for self-hosted instances`) on the GITLAB_API_URL env line so the snapshot parses as JSON; no key/value/command shape changed

humanExpectation.rationale: A pinned docker image (mcp/gitlab) launched with a GITLAB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN credential env → secrets.env-key fires (medium, S2) → REVIEW. It also pins a true-negative within the case: GITLAB_API_URL is a host/URL env name, not credential-shaped, so it must NOT add a second secrets.env-key evidence entry. No broad path (no bind mount), no unpinned package (docker image, not npx), no dangerous command.
knownLimitations: secrets.env-key keys on credential-shaped substrings (TOKEN/SECRET/KEY/PASSWORD/CREDENTIAL/AUTH). GITLAB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN matches (TOKEN); GITLAB_API_URL does not — correctly unflagged. CallLint reads env-key SHAPE, not values.
